text: MarketReel — Global Film DistributionInside OutFilm Synopsis & Narrative AnalysisMarketReel Intelligence Document 2015 Prepared for Acquisition ReviewProduction OverviewTitleInside OutYear2015DirectorPete DocterGenreAnimation / Drama / ComedyRuntime95 minStudio / DistributorPixar Animation Studios / DisneyPrincipal CastAmy Poehler, Phyllis Smith, Bill Hader, Mindy Kaling, Lewis Black, Kaitlyn DiasExecutive SynopsisWhen 11-year-old Riley's family moves across the country, the emotions inside her mind — Joy, Sadness, Fear, Anger, and Disgust — struggle to navigate a world being turned upside down.Detailed Narrative SynopsisInside Out takes place simultaneously in two worlds: the external life of 11-year-old Riley Andersen and the internal landscape of her mind, Headquarters, where her core emotions — Joy, Sadness, Fear, Anger, and Disgust — operate as distinct characters managing her emotional responses.Riley has lived a happy Minnesota childhood, with Joy effectively running Headquarters and minimizing Sadness's influence. When her family relocates to San Francisco for her father's startup, everything Riley has known is upturned. Her core memories — which define her personality "islands" (Family, Friendship, Hockey, Honesty, Goofiness) — begin to destabilize.During a conflict over a core memory, Joy and Sadness are accidentally ejected from Headquarters into the vast landscape of Long-Term Memory. Without them, only Fear, Anger, and Disgust remain in control. Riley becomes emotionally numb, eventually attempting to run away back to Minnesota.Joy and Sadness journey through Long-Term Memory, the Imagination Land, Abstract Thought, and Dream Productions, accompanied by Riley's forgotten imaginary friend Bing Bong. The film's emotional pivot occurs when Joy witnesses how a memory she'd considered purely sad is, in fact, tinged with joy — that sadness and joy are inseparable, and that Sadness's function is essential: it signals to others that help is needed.Bing Bong sacrifices himself in the Memory Dump so Joy can escape, one of the film's most affecting moments. Joy returns to HQ, allows Sadness to take control, and Riley — moved to finally express her grief to her parents — breaks down and is comforted. Her personality islands begin to rebuild, now more complex and mixed-emotion than before. The film ends with Riley's 12th birthday approaching and a mental landscape that now accommodates emotional nuance.Narrative Themes for Distribution AnalysisThe necessity of sadness and all emotionsIdentity formation during adolescenceThe complexity of emotional experienceLoss of childhoodFamily bonds under stressTone & Genre ClassificationGenre Classification: Animation / Drama / ComedyAudience Rating Profile: See Censorship & Certification Document for territory-specific ratings.Narrative Complexity: This document should be read in conjunction with the Cultural Sensitivity Report and Marketing Strategy Brief for complete acquisition analysis.MarketReel Confidential — Inside Out Synopsis Document — For Internal Distribution Use Only
